title: Seamark & Butley Priory: Sam & Iona's Wedding (Suffolk)

main_route.excerpt: On to Seamark where we camped in the garden.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page B_0335_R also contains content from a preceding trip (Berrington Hall / Monday entry)

summary: A trip from Birmingham to Seamark in Suffolk (Friday 27 September 2019) for Sam and Iona's wedding, with an overnight stay camping in the garden. The diary records a difficult departure, a stop at kennels for Tinder, and a sad lunch with John and Pamela en route; Butley Priory features in the title but lies beyond the surviving page fragment.
extraction_notes: Only one half-page of diary text survives for this trip (B_0335_R, mixed_start and mixed_end). The 'Monday' heading on the same page belongs to a different trip. The date 2019-09-27 is explicit ('Friday Sept 27th'). Travellers are Simon and Eve based on narrative voice; Tinder (dog) was kennelled. Butley Priory is in the title_guess but absent from the text — it cannot be placed as a confirmed stop. Return home is inferred convention only.
